summary: British-Zimbabwean novelist. 2007 Nobel laureate. Known for The Golden Notebook (Anna Wulf's color-coded notebooks), The Grass is Singing, Children of Violence series, The Good Terrorist, and Canopus in Argos sci-fi series.

works.name: The Golden Notebook
works.description: Novel about Anna Wulf's four color-coded notebooks (black, red, yellow, blue) with frame story 'Free Women.'

clues.clue: Anna Wulf keeps four color-coded notebooks (black, red, yellow, blue); frame story 'Free Women'
examples: Anna Wulf divides her life into subjects recorded in black, red, yellow, and blue diaries
interspersed among the frame story 'Free Women'

clues.clue: Anna wrote Frontiers of War about her experience in Rhodesia
examples: lives off money earned for her book Frontiers of War

clues.clue: Tommy's failed suicide leaves him blind
examples: Tommy's failed suicide attempt leaves him blind

works.name: The Grass is Singing
works.description: Novel about Mary Turner murdered by her servant Moses; title from The Waste Land.

clues.clue: Mary Turner murdered by servant Moses; Charlie Slatter takes over farm; title from The Waste Land
examples: Moses kills his white employer, Mary Turner
a passage in Eliot's 'The Waste Land'

clues.clue: Neighbor Slatter disgusted to see Moses dressing Mary
examples: Slatter is disgusted to see her servant Moses dressing her

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Children of Violence series, The Good Terrorist, Canopus in Argos, Alfred and Emily.

clues.clue: Children of Violence — semi-autobiographical about Martha Quest in fictional Zambesia/Zambezia
examples: Martha Quest in the fictional country of Zambezia

clues.clue: The Good Terrorist — Alice Mellings and the Communist Centre Union; car bomb
examples: Alice Mellings, which sets off a car-bomb

clues.clue: Canopus in Argos — five-book sci-fi series including Shikasta; emissary Johor
examples: the emissary Johor appears in a series including Shikasta

clues.clue: Memoirs of a Survivor — based on time with teenager Jenny Diski; Gerald and Emily
examples: partially based on the author's time living with the teenager Jenny Dicki

clues.clue: 'Through the Tunnel' — boy Jerry swims under a rock formation
examples: Jerry concludes that 'it was no longer of the least importance to go to the bay'

comprehensive_summary: Doris Lessing is a British-Zimbabwean novelist and 2007 Nobel laureate. The Golden Notebook features Anna Wulf's four color-coded notebooks framed by 'Free Women.' The Grass is Singing has Mary Turner murdered by Moses. The Children of Violence series follows Martha Quest. The Good Terrorist features Alice Mellings. Canopus in Argos is a five-book sci-fi series.

cards.front: Novel: Anna Wulf keeps four color-coded notebooks (black, red, yellow, blue); frame story 'Free Women'
Novel: Mary Turner murdered by her servant Moses
Novel: title from The Waste Land
Author: semi-autobiographical Children of Violence series about Martha Quest in fictional Zambesia
Novel: Alice Mellings and the Communist Centre Union set off a car-bomb that kills one of their own members
